title: Finance and Economics Discussion Series (FEDS) - 2025
summary: This Federal Reserve listing page indexes multiple 2025 FEDS research papers; the source is truncated mid-list. Most papers cover topics unrelated to market structure or macro catalysts (labor economics, AI, payment systems, private firm returns). One paper (FEDS 2025-106) addresses fund flow dynamics and mechanical portfolio rebalancing during COVID-19, touching on market structure concerns around fund outflow contagion. This listing page should not be archived as a single record.
key_points: FEDS 2025-110: Interest rate ceilings on small-dollar loans and labor supply effects; not market-structure relevant
FEDS 2025-109: Data center investment forecasting ($370bn annualized by 2026:Q2); not market-structure relevant
FEDS 2025-108: LLM efficiency methods (knowledge distillation); not market-structure relevant
FEDS 2025-107: Private vs public firm rates of return; not market-structure relevant
FEDS 2025-106 (partial): Funds of funds rebalancing during COVID-19; mechanical selling transmitted shocks across markets, government bond fund sales reached 34% of holdings and 100%+ of outflows
FEDS 2025-105 and beyond: Source truncated; additional papers not visible
why_it_matters: This is a listing page (not an archiveable source per rule 5). The page contains multiple distinct papers that require individual archival treatment. Each FEDS paper has a separate URL with its full text that should be archived separately. The most market-structure-relevant paper (FEDS 2025-106 on funds-of-funds rebalancing) should be archived using its own DOI URL.
market_structure_context: FEDS 2025-106 (partial data visible): During March 2020 COVID crisis, funds of funds sold 14% of total bond fund holdings, concentrating in government bond funds (34% liquidation). This demonstrates how mechanical rebalancing rules can amplify market stress and transmit shocks across asset classes. However, this observation is from a listing page—the actual paper at its dedicated URL should be archived instead.
important_numbers: 14% of total bond fund holdings sold by funds of funds (March 2020)
34% of government bond fund holdings liquidated
100%+ of outflows from government bond funds came from these sales
$370bn projected annualized data center investment by 2026:Q2
